title: nvm_write: deleted flag is redundant; use id != INVALID directly
description: In nvm.rs nvm_write, a deleted: bool flag is set inside if id != NvmId::INVALID, then the closure map_add_err checks if deleted to decide which error to surface. The flag carries no new information — deleted is always equivalent to id != NvmId::INVALID. Remove the flag; rewrite the closure as |e| if id != NvmId::INVALID { WolfHsmError::DataLost { id: id.0 } } else { e }. The deletion-then-add non-atomicity is important; the flag obscures rather than clarifies it.
